params.name: Lissajous Figure
params.description:
On Jul 31, 1857 AD, Lissajous published a paper on simple harmonic motion figures.
Based on Bowditch's research, a French mathematician named Lissajous expanded on the Lissajous figures and popularized them. Essentially, it is a regular and stable closed curve crafted by two simple harmonic vibrations at frequencies that are in simple integer ratios in directions perpendicular to each other.
